description: PR-10.2 mock exposure-receipt hardening review (companion to pr10-2-exposure-receipt.md).
method: operator adversarial self-audit of mock_exposure_receipt / render_mock / CLI render, then an INDEPENDENT agent code review dispatched and COMPLETED before commit per governance §16.0. The independent reviewer probed content-freedom, enforcement strength, burn-set agreement, CLI leak, determinism, and test quality, and verified the bypass live.

independent_findings.problem: the PR claimed render_mock made the burn obligation 'inseparable by construction' / 'no code path can surface a mock without it' / 'enforces the rule', but mock_learner_view was a PUBLIC unguarded function returning the same rendered holdout scenarios with no receipt — the reviewer verified the bypass live. The enforcement claim was overstated relative to what the code delivered.
independent_findings.resolution: raw projection renamed to a private _mock_learner_view; render_mock is now the SOLE public render surface, so the default public path carries the receipt. All 'enforces / no code path / inseparable / by construction' language in the module docstring, render_mock docstring, cli docstring, and report softened to the honest 'the sanctioned public render surface always carries the receipt (Python cannot hard-enforce; a caller can still reach the private helper).'

independent_findings.problem: no test proved a caller CANNOT bypass the receipt; the 'inseparable — you cannot get one without the other' inline comment asserted an invariant the code did not hold and no test checked.
independent_findings.resolution: added test_raw_projection_is_private_no_public_unguarded_render (asserts mock_learner_view is not a public attribute and every public render path carries a receipt); removed the overstated inline comment.

independent_findings.problem: the three-way agreement assertion compared three values that were all the return of the SAME function exposed_holdout_ids(mock) — near-tautological; could only fail if a call site were deleted, never if the burn logic diverged.
independent_findings.resolution: the expected burn set is now recomputed independently from mock['items'] (sorted holdout-lane ids) as an oracle independent of the function under test; receipt, exposed_holdout_ids, and grade_mock.burned_holdout_ids are each asserted against that oracle.

independent_findings.problem: the mock-render gate asserted only exit 0, while its comment claimed it 'proves every render path emits the content-free burn receipt'; a CLI regression that printed stems would keep the gate green.
independent_findings.resolution: the gate now captures stdout and (a) requires the exposure receipt present and (b) fails on any content/answer-bearing field (stem, choices, correct_index, answer_key_ref, rationale_refs, item_hash, distractor_misconceptions), mirroring quiz-export-scan. Applied in both the Makefile and the CI workflow step.

independent_findings.problem: the receipt counts ASSEMBLED holdout exposure while mock_learner_view drops ids that fail to resolve, so item_count/exposed set can over-report vs what actually rendered.
independent_findings.resolution: documented explicitly that the receipt intentionally counts assembled exposure — conservative, so burn can over-count but never under-count (never leaks). No behavior change; this is the safe direction.

clean_areas_confirmed_by_reviewer: receipt is content-free (verified against all 12 holdout items): only draw_key, item_count, exposed_holdout_ids, holdout_exposure_count, burn_required
item ids are the intended minimal exposure — the id is the burn key; the descriptive topic text is not in the id
burn-set agreement is airtight by shared-function construction across empty/no-holdout/no-submission/partial-submission cases (a genuine design strength; only the TEST of it was tautological — F3)
determinism: sorted({...}) over item ids is a pure order-stable function of the mock

CLI render prints only the content-free receipt to stdout and the burn warning to stderr (correct streams), exits 0; burn_required = bool(exposed) is correct in exposed and empty cases; no holdout stems reach stdout/stderr
grade_mock keeps holdout content internal; fresh_scenario_count counts only submitted holdout scenarios while burning all exposed
note: Independent review completed BEFORE this commit (the PR-9 lesson). Verdict accept-with-fixes; all five findings applied before commit. The initial overstated enforcement claim (F1) was corrected in code and prose rather than left standing.
